function.name: code_run
function.description: Code execution tool. Prefer python. Supports Multi-call, use script parameter when parallel. Without script parameter, body code block will be executed. For single calls, prefer code block to avoid escaping issues. Do not hardcode large amounts of data.

script.description: [Optional] Code to execute. To avoid escaping, leave empty and use body code block instead (mutually exclusive with this parameter).

cwd.description: Working directory, defaults to current working directory

function.name: file_read
function.description: Read file contents. Recommended to read before modifying to ensure latest context and line numbers. Supports paginated reading or keyword search.

path.description: Relative or absolute path to file

keyword.description: Optional search keyword. If provided, returns the first match (case-insensitive) and surrounding lines.

show_linenos.description: Whether to show line numbers, recommended to assist file_patch targeting

function.name: file_patch
function.description: Fine-grained local file modification. Finds unique old_content block and replaces with new_content. old_content must uniquely exist in the file, spaces/indent/newlines must perfectly match the original. If match fails, use file_read to re-confirm.

old_content.description: Original text block to be replaced (must ensure uniqueness)

new_content.description: New replacement text. Supports {{file:path:startLine:endLine}} syntax to reference file content, auto auto-expands before writing

function.name: file_write
function.description: For creating new files, full overwrite, or append writing. For fine-grained code edits, prefer file_patch. Note: content to write must be placed in <file_content> tag or code block in response body. Content supports {{file:path:startLine:endLine}} syntax.

mode.description: Write mode: overwrite, append, or prepend

function.name: web_scan
function.description: Get simplified HTML content and tab list of current page. Removes hidden/floating/covered elements. Should generally scan after switching pages.

tabs_only.description: Only return tab list and current tab info, do not fetch HTML content

switch_tab_id.description: Optional tab ID. If provided, system will switch to it before scanning

text_only.description: Return only plain text, no HTML

function.name: web_execute_js
function.description: Execute JS. Supports multi-call, use different switch_tab_id for parallel multi-tab ops. Do not guess, operate accurately to reduce web_scan calls. Without script parameter, executes body ```javascript block.

script.description: [Optional] JS code or path. To avoid escaping, use body code block instead.

save_to_file.description: Save result to file, suitable for long returns

no_monitor.description: Skip page change monitor, saves 2-3s. Only set when purely reading info, do not set when operating page.

switch_tab_id.description: Optional tab ID to switch to before execution

function.name: update_working_checkpoint
function.description:
Short-term working note, auto-injected each turn to prevent info loss in long tasks. Call early-to-mid, not at end. When to call: (1) After reading SOP at start, save user needs & key constraints (except for simple 1-2 step tasks); (2) Switching subtasks or before context clears; (3) After multiple retries fail, re-read SOP and must call to store new finds; (4) Switching to new task to reset progress but keep valid constraints.

When not to call: Simple tasks, or task is finished (should use long-term update).

key_info.description: Replace current note (<200 tokens). Incremental update: review existing, keep valid, then add/edit/del. Store pitfalls, original needs, key params/finds, file paths, progress, next plan. Do not store immediate throw-away info, obvious contexts, or old task info when entirely switched tasks.

related_sop.description: Related SOP names, can be multiple, re-read when necessary

function.name: ask_user
function.description: Call this to interrupt task and ask questions when user decision is needed, extra info is required, or encountering unresolvable blockers.

question.description: Clear question posed to the user

candidates.description: List of optional quick-choice options for the user

function.name: start_long_term_update
function.description: Prepare to distill memory. Call when finding info worth long-term memory (environment facts/user preferences/pitfall experience). No need to call if already updated or inside auto-flow. Must call for tasks taking >15 turns to precipitate experience.
